advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the reviewer can actually resolve the recursive depends_on closure and ancestor deliverables from its available context/tools; if not, provide that context or scope the carve-out to what the plan text can support.
evidence: plan_review_G1G2.md:10: "Verify (via Glob/Grep) that every file/symbol the plan names actually exists ... Any symbol created by a ticket this ticket depends_on (evaluated recursively) is treated as if it EXISTS and is NOT MISSING."
plan_review_finder.md:30-31: '# Plan under review (verbatim, whole)\n{{plan}}' — only the current plan text is injected, not the transitive dependency closure or ancestor deliverables.
Ticket root cause: 'neither aware of the ticket dependency graph' — the fix is prose only and adds no graph/tool affordance.
advisory.finding: The carve-out instructs the reviewer to treat symbols created by a depends_on ancestor 'evaluated recursively' as existing, but the G1G2/verifier tooling is Glob/Grep over the codebase and the finder is fed only the current plan verbatim ({{plan}}); no tool clearly provides the recursive ticket-dependency graph or each ancestor's deliverable list, so the directive is under-supported and may be either ineffective or over-applied.
scenarios: Reviewer cannot enumerate the recursive depends_on closure from plan text alone, so either keeps flagging (no effect) or assumes any absent symbol 'is probably from a dependency', suppressing genuine hallucinations/forward references (false negatives).

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the FP case mode to something like 'upstream-dependency-provided-artifact' to avoid the misleading 'forward' term.
evidence: Ticket: 'Forward references (to DOWNSTREAM/sibling deliverables) ... remain a VALID finding ... the carve-out is scoped to UPSTREAM dependencies only.' — upstream is the opposite of forward.
The recall case (line 408) is the true forward reference; naming the upstream case '...forward-artifact' is confusing.
advisory.finding: The false-fire eval case id FP-G1G2-upstream-dep-provided-symbol uses mode 'upstream-dependency-forward-artifact', but 'forward-artifact' contradicts the case's intent — an upstream (depends_on) deliverable is explicitly NOT a forward reference per the ticket's own distinction.
scenarios: A maintainer reading the taxonomy mode conflates the upstream (should-pass) case with a forward reference (should-fire), inverting the intended semantics.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a G1G2 {true: forward-ref missing-X upheld; false: upstream-dependency missing-X downgraded} discrimination pair to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml to cover the new verifier carve-out.
evidence: Diff appends to verifier: 'Any symbol created by a ticket this ticket depends_on (evaluated recursively) is treated as if it EXISTS and is NOT MISSING.' (plan_review_verifier_agentic.md:33-34)

plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:18-24 scorer discriminates_true_from_false: 'For each {true, planted-false} pair, the planted-false finding's graded validity ... is STRICTLY LOWER than the true finding's' — the harness built to guard this exact behavior
search_files 'depends_on' in plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml -> (no matches); search_files 'absent' -> (no matches): no upstream-dependency / absence discrimination pair exists
New eval cases were added only to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml (G1G2 finder), covering the finder surface but not the modified verifier surface
advisory.finding: The Pass-2 agentic verifier gains a new behavioral carve-out (an upstream-dependency 'missing X' finding must be downgraded to evidence_entails_finding=no) but no eval case exercises it. Its dedicated discrimination harness plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml — whose scorer 'discriminates_true_from_false' exists precisely to verify the verifier knocks down planted-false 'missing X' findings while upholding true ones — receives no G1G2 upstream-dependency pair. The verifier-side behavior change is regression-blind.
scenarios: A Pass-1 finder (bespoke or non-carve-out variant) still emits an upstream-dependency 'missing X' finding; the Pass-2 verifier is the second line of defense meant to knock it down — but no eval proves it does, so a regression that lets it rubber-stamp such a finding ships undetected.
A future edit to the verifier's ABSENCE/'missing X' paragraph silently drops the carve-out with no failing test.
advisory.suggested_fix: In plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml, add a paired dataset entry on criterion G1G2: a `kind: false` case where a 'missing X' finding targets a symbol an upstream depends_on ancestor creates (expect: low_validity), paired with a `kind: true` forward-reference case (ADR-0042 shape, no upstream producer; expect: high_validity).

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the Pass-2 verifier upstream-dependency carve-out.
coaching.subject: the Pass-2 verifier upstream-dependency carve-out
